deployment_question: How does the model perform on future data relative to the training period?
definition: Train on earlier observations and evaluate on later observations, optionally with rolling or expanding windows. This targets the deployment point created by temporal drift.

split_operator.spectra_mapping: future samples have zero future-to-train leakage and increasing temporal distance

implementation_recipe: Sort samples by timestamp or experimental acquisition order.
Choose a cutoff, rolling window, or n-step-forward protocol.
Train only on data available before the test period.
quality_gates: Confirm preprocessing and feature construction use only pre-cutoff information.
Report temporal gaps, period sizes, and label prevalence by period.
Compare with the SPECTRA curve over temporal distance or feature drift.
failure_modes: Feature engineering can leak future aggregate statistics.
Seasonality or intervention effects can confound temporal novelty with label shifts.
A single cutoff can be unrepresentative.

citations.title: Validation of prediction models: examining temporal and geographic stability
citations.evidence: Evaluates temporal and geographic stability of clinical prediction models.

citations.title: Step Forward Cross Validation for Bioactivity Prediction
citations.evidence: Uses k-fold n-step-forward validation to evaluate out-of-distribution bioactivity prediction.
